title: India has 100M weekly active ChatGPT users, Sam Altman says
description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman says India has the largest number of student users of ChatGPT worldwide.

title: The enterprise AI land grab is on. Glean is building the layer beneath the interface.
description: In this week's episode of the Equity podcast, Glean CEO Arvind Jain explains the company's shift from enterprise search tool to middleware layer for enterprise AI.

title: Hollywood isn’t happy about the new Seedance 2.0 video generator
description: Hollywood organizations are pushing back against a new AI video model called Seedance 2.0, which they say has quickly become a tool for “blatant” copyright infringement.

title: The great computer science exodus (and where students are going instead)
description: Students are losing some interest in computer science broadly but gaining interest in AI-specific majors and courses.

title: Is safety ‘dead’ at xAI?
description: Elon Musk is “actively” working to make xAI’s Grok chatbot “more unhinged, according to a former employee.

title: In a changed VC landscape, this exec is doubling down on overlooked founders
description: As much of Silicon Valley chases mega-rounds and buzzy AI deals, Stacy Brown-Philpot is running Cherryrock Capital like a throwback to venture capital's earlier days

title: Designer Kate Barton teams up with IBM and Fiducia AI for a NYFW presentation
description: Designer Kate Barton teams up with Fiducia AI and IBM for a NYFW presentation.

title: Nothing opens its first retail store in India
description: The two-story location will sell products from Nothing and the more affordable, mass-market brand CMF.

title: Indian pharmacy chain giant exposed customer data and internal systems
description: A backend flaw in web admin dashboards used by one of India's largest pharmacy chains, exposed thousands of online pharmacy orders.

title: Airbnb plans to bake in AI features for search, discovery and support
description: Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky said the company wants to increase its use of large language models for customer discovery, support and engineering.

title: Airbnb says a third of its customer support is now handled by AI in the US and Canada
description: Airbnb was poised to introduce an app that doesn't just search for you, but one that "knows you." CEO Brian Chesky said, "It will help guests plan their entire trip, help hosts better run their businesses, and help the company operate more efficiently at scale."

title: Fintech lending giant Figure confirms data breach
description: The company said hackers downloaded “a limited number of files” after breaking into an employee’s account. The hacking group ShinyHunters took responsibility for the breach.

title: Why top talent is walking away from OpenAI and xAI
description: <![CDATA[AI companies have been hemorrhaging talent the past few weeks. Half of xAI’s founding team has left the company — some on their own, others through “restructuring” — while OpenAI is facing its own shakeups, from the disbanding of its mission alignment team

title: Sex toys maker Tenga says hacker stole customer information
description: The Japanese sex toy maker said a hacker broke into an employee's inbox and stole customer names, email addresses, and correspondence, including order details and customer service inquiries.

title: I hate my AI pet with every fiber of my being
Apple’s first-gen AirTags are still worth buying now that they’re $16 apiece
Why are Epstein’s emails full of equals signs?
AI can’t make good video game worlds yet, and it might never be able to
Jikipedia turns Epstein’s emails into an encyclopedia of his powerful friends
